tasks.instruction: Przeczytaj ponizszy tekst. Zdecyduj, ktore zdania (1.1-1.8) sa zgodne z trescia tekstu (T – True), ktore sa niezgodne (F – False), a ktore zawieraja informacje niepodane w tekscie (NI – No Information).
tasks.text:
MARIE CURIE

Marie Curie was born Maria Sklodowska in Warsaw, Poland, in 1867. As a young woman, she dreamed of studying science, but at that time women were not allowed to attend university in Poland. Determined to get an education, she moved to Paris in 1891 and enrolled at the Sorbonne, one of the oldest universities in Europe.

Life in Paris was not easy for Marie. She had very little money and sometimes could not afford to buy food. Despite these difficulties, she studied extremely hard and graduated at the top of her class in physics in 1893. A year later, she also earned a degree in mathematics.

In Paris, Marie met Pierre Curie, a French physicist. They married in 1895 and began working together in a small, poorly equipped laboratory. Together, they researched radioactive materials and discovered two new chemical elements: polonium, which Marie named after her home country Poland, and radium. Their work was groundbreaking but also dangerous — they did not know that the radiation they were exposed to every day was harmful to their health.

In 1903, Marie and Pierre Curie, along with Henri Becquerel, received the Nobel Prize in Physics. This made Marie the first woman ever to win a Nobel Prize. After Pierre died in a road accident in 1906, Marie continued her research alone. In 1911, she won a second Nobel Prize, this time in Chemistry, becoming the first person to win Nobel Prizes in two different scientific fields.

Marie Curie died in 1934 from a blood disease almost certainly caused by her long exposure to radiation. Her notebooks are still so radioactive that they must be kept in lead-lined boxes, and anyone who wishes to read them must wear protective clothing.

items.statement: Marie Curie was unable to study at university in Poland because women were not permitted to do so.
Marie had a comfortable and well-funded life when she first arrived in Paris.
Marie earned her physics degree before her mathematics degree.
Marie and Pierre had two daughters together.
The element polonium was named after Pierre Curie's birthplace.
Marie Curie was the first person to receive Nobel Prizes in two different sciences.
Marie Curie's research laboratory in Paris has been turned into a museum.
People can freely handle Marie Curie's original notebooks without any special precautions.
